title: The Zohar
hook: The Zohar. Ten sefirot — ten emanations of divine speech.

sections.heading: Opening
sections.narration: The Zohar. Ten sefirot — ten emanations of divine speech.
sections.visualHint: Slow zoom on illuminated manuscript / symbolic stone — kabbalah color accent.

sections.heading: Context
sections.narration: In 1300 CE, within the Kabbalah stream of spiritual history — at Human scale — a door opens.
sections.visualHint: Era card: 1300 CE; tradition motif in kabbalah palette; subtle symbolic geometry.

sections.heading: The mystery
sections.narration: Attributed to Moses de León in medieval Spain, the Zohar is a vast mystical commentary on Torah cast as wanderings of Rabbi Shimon bar Yochai.
sections.visualHint: Abstract esoteric imagery — kabbalah; textural, luminous, not literal.

sections.narration: Its mythopoetic language of sefirot, shekhinah, and exile will define Kabbalah for centuries and influence Christian Kabbalists and Hermetic orders.
sections.visualHint: Abstract esoteric imagery — kabbalah; textural, luminous, not literal.

sections.heading: Cosmos
sections.narration: Learn more from the sources cited in Cosmos. Zoom out to the universe. Zoom in to walk the stones. The hidden streams wait.
sections.visualHint: Cosmos wordmark; starfield fading to a single glowing stone; contemplative outro.

openMontage.cursorPrompt:
Using the OpenMontage Animated Explainer pipeline, produce a 60-second video from the attached Cosmos brief.

- Use Piper TTS for narration (free/local path unless I provide other API keys).
- Use Remotion image scenes with Ken Burns motion — contemplative pacing.
- Tone: contemplative. Style playbook: clean_professional.
- Accent color for titles and transitions: #d4a843.
- Follow the 5 sections in the brief (hook → context → core → links → close).
- Honor onScreenText and visualHint fields for each section.
